steps.text: The graph displays $y = f(x) + 14$. Read two points from the graphed line: the y-intercept appears at $(0, 2)$ and another point at $(-4, 3)$.
Compute the slope: $$m = \frac{3-2}{-4-0} = \frac{1}{-4} = -\frac{1}{4}$$ With y-intercept 2, the graphed line is $y = -\frac{1}{4}x + 2$.
Since the graph shows $f(x) + 14$, set equal and isolate $f(x)$: $$f(x) + 14 = -\frac{1}{4}x + 2 \implies f(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x + 2 - 14 = -\frac{1}{4}x - 12$$

• A — $f(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x - 12$ matches the derived expression exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B — $f(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x + 16$ would require the graphed line to have y-intercept 30, inconsistent with the graph. Eliminated.
  • C — $f(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x + 2$ forgets to subtract 14 from the graphed function. Eliminated.
  • D — $f(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x - 14$ would require the graphed line to have y-intercept 0, inconsistent with the graph. Eliminated.
